116308.explanation: The CDA believes that the executive board should consist of people suitable for the portfolio.

116309.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of referendums.

116311.explanation: The CDA wants to gladly support farmers who want to switch (to other methods/practices).

116312.explanation: The CDA wants to support residents who want to jointly generate sustainable energy.

116313.explanation: The CDA believes that assigned asylum seekers should be accommodated.

116315.explanation: The CDA believes companies have a responsibility for seasonal workers they bring to the Netherlands.

116319.explanation: The CDA wants to keep the option for fireworks on New Year's Eve, even with a private fireworks ban. This can be done because the municipality itself organizes a fireworks show or allows fireworks to be set off in association contexts. The CDA also considers carbide shooting part of our New Year's tradition.

116325.explanation: The CDA considers a local broadcaster very important.

116327.explanation: The CDA wants sport and culture to be accessible to all children. We do this in cooperation with our associations.

116331.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. More cameras contribute to this.

116332.explanation: The CDA believes that victims of domestic violence deserve the help and support they need. Thanks to a refuge shelter, vulnerable women, children and other victims receive assistance, efforts are made to stop the violence, and temporary safety can be provided.

116336.explanation: The CDA supports 'one more street' and 'one more neighborhood'. This approach ensures livability in every center and hamlet.

116338.explanation: The municipality must show its human side: waiving municipal taxes is one of the most effective measures municipalities can take to support people with low incomes.

116339.explanation: The CDA wants to support people who fall just above the income limit as well.

116341.explanation: The CDA considers it important to have sufficient charging points for electric cars. These should be placed based on demand, where people with electric cars live and work. Residents should be able to submit an application to the municipality for this.

116342.explanation: The CDA wants you to be able to drive safely at 50 km/h on the main roads within urban areas. The standard in residential streets is 30 km/h.

116343.explanation: The CDA values choice options in healthcare. Sometimes the differences in rates are so large or the quality so low that it is undesirable for every care provider to be funded by the municipality.

116350.explanation: Nothing is free. Waste processing costs money, for which we pay a waste levy. The CDA wants to keep these levies as low as possible.

116355.explanation: The CDA does not want any coffeeshops in the municipality.

116357.explanation: The CDA believes that tall apartment buildings do not fit in Hof van Twente.

116358.explanation: Part of society needs room for renting out individual rooms. Think, for example, of students or young people. There must be space for this.

116360.explanation: The CDA wants more long-term parking spaces in Markelo.

116362.explanation: The CDA believes that the municipality should continue discussions with ABZ to explore possibilities for relocating the company.

116363.explanation: The area near homes is too broad a concept. The CDA stands for a healthy living environment but also for a healthy agricultural sector. A possible ban is not currently under consideration.

116365.explanation: The CDA has always been a proponent of the family subscription and believes it should be reinstated.

parties.name: GroenLinks-PvdA-InBeweging
parties.fullName: Groenlinks - Partij van de Arbeid - InBeweging

116308.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality, with an equal number of men and women. Such a government represents all perspectives and opinions to make good decisions.

116309.explanation: Residents must have real influence. This can sometimes be achieved through a referendum, but especially through permanent forms of participation, such as citizen assemblies and citizen motions. This way, residents are involved from start to finish in decisions, strengthening local democracy.

116311.explanation: Side activities such as a farm shop or a small-scale camping site can contribute to the income and future prospects of farmers. We want to make this possible, provided it fits the environment, is sustainable, and occurs in good consultation with the municipality and neighbors.

116312.explanation: Residents collectively generate their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity at the same location, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives like Hof van Twente op Rozen are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

116313.explanation: It is our social responsibility to offer people a safe place. Hof van Twente will continue to provide temporary shelter to comply with the national distribution obligation.

116315.explanation: Those who bring people to work in Hof van Twente must ensure good housing. This must not cause nuisance to neighbors. There is no place for exploitation or abuses in our municipality.

116319.explanation: We celebrate the New Year's party together. For many people, fireworks are part of the tradition. With a large fireworks show organized by the municipality, we maintain this tradition but in a safer and more sociable way. Additional events allow everyone to participate, including the elderly and people with pets.

116325.explanation: Local media are incredibly important to know what is happening in your own area, from the street to the municipal council. But many local media struggle to stay afloat. We believe the municipality should contribute to this.

116327.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

116331.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

116332.explanation: For people who are no longer safe at home, there must always be a place to go. A women's shelter is indispensable for victims of domestic violence—women, men, and children, regardless of age or background. Safety and protection always come first.

116335.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution for the housing shortage. But as long as the need is high, some people can use them. It is important that they can use the holiday home as a fixed address for as long as necessary.

116336.explanation: The municipality should mainly build within or directly adjacent to villages. This keeps the landscape open and villages livable. Building outside village boundaries is only allowed if it is small-scale and residents can participate in the decision.

116337.explanation: People on social assistance are usually not there voluntarily. They first need rest, certainty, and trust. Mandatory work often causes extra stress and does not help people find a job faster. We prefer to activate rather than oblige.

116338.explanation: If debt forgiveness of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support debt forgiveness.

116339.explanation: In some families, the income is just too high to qualify for assistance, but they still do not have enough money to make ends meet. Therefore, we want to raise the income threshold. This way, more people struggling can receive support.

116341.explanation: Everyone should be able to charge their car, including those without a private driveway. Therefore, we want more parking spaces that also function as charging spots. This gives everyone the same opportunity to drive electrically and together we ensure a cleaner living environment.

116342.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

116343.explanation: People must feel safe and understood in healthcare. Therefore, it is important that they, where possible, can choose a healthcare provider that suits them. At the same time, good care must remain available to everyone.

116348.explanation: In our villages and centers, it is important that facilities remain accessible. Therefore, we believe stores should be allowed to open on Sundays if they wish. This helps residents who work during the week and supports local entrepreneurs.

116350.explanation: Litter on the street is annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. It is much better if people bring their waste to be properly sorted and processed by the municipality. This results in clean streets and a healthy environment. Therefore, we believe the recycling center should remain free for private individuals.

116354.explanation: The Twents dialect is part of the culture and history of Hof van Twente. By paying attention to Twents in primary schools, children learn more about their environment and background. Schools decide how to do this themselves, with support from the municipality.

116355.explanation: People in Hof van Twente also use soft drugs. Allowing a coffee shop enables the municipality to set rules for safety, health, and nuisance. This prevents illegal trade and ensures supervision, with attention to the neighborhood.

116357.explanation: We need more housing, also in Hof van Twente. Sometimes building upwards can help. At the same time, new homes must fit the character of villages and centers. Therefore, we choose the best fit for each location.

116358.explanation: We face a huge housing shortage, and many people in our municipality live in more space than necessary. Therefore, we want to make it easier to rent out individual rooms, provided they are well maintained and the rent is fair.

116360.explanation: In Markelo, it is important that the village remains pleasant and safe. More parking spots do not always solve that. We prefer smart solutions, such as better use of existing parking and safe routes for cyclists and pedestrians. Better traffic flow is the priority.

116362.explanation: Living and health come first. Heavy industry does not belong in the middle of a residential area. The municipality must actively pursue the relocation of ABZ from Markelo, with attention to care and good solutions for all involved.

116363.explanation: People must be able to live safely and healthily. The use of agricultural chemicals near homes can pose health risks. Therefore, we want to ban their use near houses, schools, and playgrounds. We seek safe and feasible solutions together with farmers.

116365.explanation: Swimming must remain affordable for families. With a family subscription, parents and children can exercise and relax together. By making this possible again at Zwembad Markelo, we ensure equal opportunities, health, and enjoyment for all families.

116308.explanation: Quality is more important. Distribution is desirable.

116311.explanation: Depends greatly on the situation, type of company, environment, and the planned enterprise.

116312.explanation: In the form of a catalyst subsidy or revolving fund.

116313.explanation: We want to be a social municipality where neighborly relations are highly valued. Shelters must be appropriate to the size of the communities.

116319.explanation: Such an initiative must come from the residents.

116336.explanation: Is not feasible given the current housing shortage.

116337.explanation: If one is capable of doing so.

116339.explanation: The threshold is already at 120%, and customization is possible.

116341.explanation: Charging points are available on request, while a considerable number are already present.

116343.explanation: If available within the municipality's care offering.

116350.explanation: This is already partly the case, and we uphold the principle that the polluter pays.

116354.explanation: Given the shortcomings in reading comprehension, we find it important to focus on this.

116355.explanation: Not appropriate in a municipality of our size.

116357.explanation: In certain situations, this must be possible.

116358.explanation: To prevent poor living conditions or exploitation, clear preconditions must be formulated.

116360.explanation: In new construction, parking spaces must be created according to the applicable standards.

116362.explanation: The authority to compel ABZ does not lie with the municipality.

116363.explanation: We are dependent on existing laws and regulations in this regard.

116365.explanation: For families with financial difficulties, we offer support to participate in social activities.

116308.explanation: The VVD values equality greatly, but it is important for us that the right people occupy the right positions. Competence is more important to VVD than gender, and the college is established based on that.

116309.explanation: In the Netherlands, we use indirect democracy. Residents can elect their representatives nationally and locally. These representatives are fully capable of performing their role; a referendum is not necessary.

116311.explanation: Opening a campsite or shop involves many complications. To prevent degradation of the rural area, we must ensure that the right businesses remain in the right places. A shop related to farming is allowed; other shops belong in the shopping street.

116312.explanation: We must continue to stimulate and help facilitate sustainability initiatives, for example through sustainability loans.

116313.explanation: As a municipality, we have a legal duty to fulfill. But the way we provide asylum seeker reception is our own choice. We support small-scale and safe reception where integration into Dutch culture plays a major role.

116315.explanation: Employers already have enough administrative work. We want to give entrepreneurs more freedom, not burden them with extra tasks and responsibilities. Housing people on private property often creates safety risks.

116319.explanation: Associations may possibly receive an exemption to organize small-scale fireworks shows. The VVD believes associations should have a choice in this, and it should not be arranged by the municipality.

116325.explanation: The local broadcaster Hofstreek FM strives to remain a local station rather than merge into a Twente regional broadcaster. We support this and do our best to facilitate it. Final decisions on this are not made by the municipality but at the national level.

116327.explanation: Music is part of artistic orientation, a subject that all primary schools are legally required to offer according to the core objectives of primary education. This means the municipality's role is not additionally needed.

116331.explanation: Vandalism and damage cause high costs in our municipality. If we install cameras at hotspots (e.g., stations), we can claim damages from the perpetrators. This way, criminals pay, not innocent residents.

116332.explanation: A women's shelter is extremely important, but anonymity is harder to find in Hof van Twente than in a city. Such initiatives should be addressed at the Twente regional level to provide the safest possible environment.

116335.explanation: We allow permanent residence in holiday homes where possible. This contributes to solving the housing crisis. It is not always the best option, so we must critically assess where it is feasible.

116336.explanation: Infill development always takes precedence over expansion, but the possibility must certainly exist.

116337.explanation: We want people receiving social assistance to reintegrate into the labor market as quickly as possible. It is therefore important that this group does not remain inactive at home but continues to contribute to society.

116338.explanation: Everyone should contribute to municipal costs to maintain facilities.

116339.explanation: Those who cannot do anything about their situation must be helped. But it is the municipality's task to help people out of poverty. Raising the income threshold does not contribute to that.

116341.explanation: If you have a gasoline car, you must go to the gas station yourself. If you have an electric car, you are likewise responsible for charging it yourself. The municipality has enough public charging points available.

116342.explanation: The maximum speed must be determined per road based on the road's design. If it is safe to drive 50 km/h within built-up areas, that is fine. Where it becomes unsafe, it must be reduced to 30 km/h.

116343.explanation: The Netherlands has a good healthcare system where everyone can get the help they need. There is a wide choice of healthcare providers.

116348.explanation: Entrepreneurs in Hof van Twente should decide themselves when they are open.

116350.explanation: If you arrange waste collection, it must also be paid for.

116354.explanation: The Twente dialect is passed down by parents and the environment. It should not be taught at school.

116355.explanation: At the Twente regional level, there is an agreement whereby the three cities (Almelo, Enschede, and Hengelo) allow coffeeshops. This arrangement has worked well so far, and we see no reason to change it.

116357.explanation: To build many homes, high-rise construction is very important within built-up areas. This provides many houses on a smaller amount of square meters.

116358.explanation: With the current housing demand, including singles, various solutions are needed. Renting out a single room should be possible if there is supply and demand. It is important that the livability of the situation is monitored.

116360.explanation: We must provide enough parking spaces for residents and visitors throughout the municipality. The option of parking garages still needs investigation, and we will encourage this.

116362.explanation: We would like to see ABZ leave the Markelo village center. However, there must be funding for this, and we are dependent on both the province and the company itself. Without financial means, we cannot compel anyone.

116363.explanation: There are national regulations for this that are constantly evaluated.

116365.explanation: The municipality does not control the rates of sports funds.

116308.explanation: The starting point is suitability for performing the function. More women are certainly possible as well.

116309.explanation: The subject must be suitable for a referendum, with sufficient turnout and initially advisory in nature.

116311.explanation: 'Always' will not always apply; consideration must be given to integration within the Hof structure.

116313.explanation: We have a good policy of distribution.

116315.explanation: That leads to 'exploitation' and 'Polish' situations. We must prevent that.

116319.explanation: That would be a fantastic idea. A beautiful connecting action.

116325.explanation: Local broadcasters connect the municipality with its citizens.

116331.explanation: Research shows that more does not yield more. It feels counterintuitive, but it is scientifically proven that camera surveillance is not more effective.

116332.explanation: When we as a municipality can support surrounding municipalities in this.

116335.explanation: This undermines the tourist character and opportunities within the municipality.

116336.explanation: Within the rules, that should be possible.

116350.explanation: Free for, for example, the first three times, then pay.

116354.explanation: That decision is up to the schools themselves.

116357.explanation: Perhaps not desirable, but when it fits, we do not want to make it impossible.

116358.explanation: To prevent 'Polish situations,' enforcement and control are extremely difficult and increase costs.

116362.explanation: However, in close consultation with the company and the province. It is certainly desirable.

116365.explanation: However, only for the family (parents) and with/for children under 13.

116308.explanation: BBB believes that the people most suited should be eligible for that.

116309.explanation: BBB is relatively positive about the idea of referenda, but it is not black and white, and there is political debate about how exactly it should be implemented.

116311.explanation: BBB is positive about the idea of a shop at or connected to a farm, especially when it concerns: selling local and regional products directly to consumers; strengthening the bond between farmers and citizens; sustainability and rural development through sales opportunities.

116312.explanation: BBB is not fundamentally against local sustainable energy. BBB is positive about facilitating local energy projects. It is not necessary for the municipality to provide financial support. Moreover, there are several subsidy options from other sources.

116313.explanation: The genuine asylum seeker must receive shelter. No large-scale asylum accommodation and no implementation of the dispersion law in Hof van Twente. No priority in housing allocation. Status holders receive no priority when assigning housing.

116315.explanation: BBB supports good housing for our migrant workers. Preferably on or near the grounds where they work. Where possible and necessary, we provide opportunities for leisure activities to prevent nuisance.

116319.explanation: BBB supports central fireworks shows as a good compromise: safer, less nuisance, and yet preserving tradition.

116325.explanation: BBB supports municipalities in supporting local broadcasters. It sees local media as an essential part of community spirit, democracy, and livability, advocating for practical, affordable support without bureaucratic burden.

116327.explanation: BBB believes every child should have the chance to take music lessons, even if parents cannot afford it themselves. Municipalities may provide subsidies or support for this, especially via local initiatives and collaboration with associations.

116331.explanation: BBB would support camera surveillance in Hof van Twente if it contributes to safety and addressing nuisance but emphasizes it must be targeted and proportional—not everywhere and not arbitrarily. BBB wants safety with common sense: protection without patronizing.

116332.explanation: BBB believes that victims of domestic violence should always have immediate access to safe shelter. In Hof van Twente, this must be available—either through a dedicated women's shelter or a well-functioning regional facility.

116335.explanation: BBB believes the municipality should allow holiday homes to be used as a primary residence, provided this is done safely, responsibly, and location-based. In this way, vacant or underused homes can contribute to extra housing and livable villages—with common sense, not with strict rules.

116336.explanation: BBB would not want a total ban on new construction outside existing village boundaries but advocates for tailored solutions, careful consideration, and appropriate building. First, make use of what is possible within the village core, but allow expansion when necessary to maintain livability and housing stock.

116337.explanation: BBB believes people receiving social assistance can do socially meaningful work as a way to remain involved and give back to the community. But this must be voluntary or customized—not as punishment or a bureaucratic obligation, but based on respect, motivation, and mutual benefit.

116338.explanation: BBB does not believe municipalities should stop waiving municipal taxes for low-income people. The party advocates for tailored solutions and social justice: help for those in need, control against abuse, and a government that acts wisely and humanely.

116339.explanation: BBB believes the municipality should raise the income threshold for poverty assistance so that people with modest incomes can also receive support. This prevents working elderly, small business owners, and families from falling through the cracks. Providing help to those who truly need it.

116341.explanation: More charging points are good, provided this is done with common sense, local customization, and without nuisance or uneven distribution of parking spaces.

116342.explanation: BBB supports 30 km/h zones where it truly improves traffic safety but opposes a general reduction for all roads within built-up areas.

116343.explanation: The party advocates for genuine freedom of choice, customized solutions, and respect for personal values in local healthcare policy.

116348.explanation: BBB is not fundamentally against or in favor of shopping on Sundays, but believes the municipality should decide this itself based on local support.

116350.explanation: BBB believes that dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free or at least not form a barrier. This encourages residents to separate their waste properly and prevents illegal dumping.

116354.explanation: It is a way to keep regional language, pride, and culture alive, and to show children where they come from. BBB advocates voluntary participation, local cooperation, and practical support.

116355.explanation: In Hof van Twente, this is only allowed if it demonstrably contributes to safety, livability, and control, without nuisance or risks for young people.

116357.explanation: BBB is not necessarily against taller residential buildings, but believes this should only be permitted in locations that fit the scale, landscape, and needs of Hof van Twente.

116358.explanation: BBB believes the municipality should allow room rentals provided it is orderly, safe, and respects the living environment. This way, residents, students, and starters can find housing more easily, while maintaining balance in neighborhoods and villages through clear local rules and supervision.

116360.explanation: BBB believes there should be more parking spaces in Markelo if research and consultation with residents show there is a genuine need. The party opts for practical solutions that suit the village, preserving safety, village character, and livability.

116362.explanation: BBB opposes forcing the animal feed factory ABZ to leave Markelo. BBB prefers consultation, innovation, and collaboration to achieve relocation rather than eviction.

116363.explanation: BBB is against a municipal ban on agricultural pesticides near houses. The party wants to work according to national regulations, science, and common sense, allowing room for innovation, dialogue, and customized solutions, but without local bans that unnecessarily restrict farmers.

116365.explanation: BBB believes the municipality may invest money in bringing back the family subscription at Markelo Swimming Pool, as this contributes to healthy and involved families.

statements.title: The municipality should ban the use of agricultural pesticides near homes.
The residents of Hof van Twente should be able to express their opinions on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Hof van Twente moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: There should be more CCTV surveillance in Hof van Twente.

titleParts.text: In Hof van Twente moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality of Hof van Twente should force animal feed factory ABZ to leave Markelo.
There should be a women's shelter (a refuge for victims of domestic violence) in Hof van Twente.

titleParts.text: In Hof van Twente moet een

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
More parking spaces should be provided in Markelo.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: More tall residential apartment blocks may be allowed in Hof van Twente.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to Twents.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should invest money to bring back the family subscription at the Markelo swimming pool.
